observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as
this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and
determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial
responsibilities;
 Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on
PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately
thereafter; and Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers
(Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with
property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able
to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
 The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early
issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. Thereafter, require the
municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements
(BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of
COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
 Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD
1445.

Recommendation:
 Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on
Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of
cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
 Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report
thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books
of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth
day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in
COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote
Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the
proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the
implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct
advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater
role for men in raising a family.

We recommended that:
a. The municipal accountant should be required to record all consummated
procurement thru the inventory account and properly follow the steps of
recording incoming and out-going supplies and materials in a perpetual
method of inventory keeping. This will prevent purchases in bulk be charged
to outright expense; and
b. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer must maintain Stock Cards as required for
in Section 119 of NGAS Manual Vol. I for local government units.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various
Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology
Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project
requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should direct the procuring department to notify
the Audit Team Leader or any of its representatives on any procurement of supplies,
materials and office equipment consummated for the agency’s operational purposes
during the procuring period within 24 hours after acceptance thereof.

Recommendation:
 We recommend that management should require the accountable officer to
issue checks in numbered sequence and prepare the RAAF monthly to
strengthen its internal control system on cash management and facilitate our
review of the financial transaction of the agency.
 In the preparation of the report, follow the prescribed form presented on
GAM for NGAs, Volume II, Appendix 67.

We recommended that:
 The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement
vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by
the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month.
Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries
and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the
requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section
122(2),P.D. 1445);
 Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent
work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
 Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents,
to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the Municipal Government of Panamao to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 noted the herein Part II audit Finding recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that officials concerned and its local Bids and Award Committee personnel to regularly and to adhere to proper internal processes by providing the resident auditor in advance with copies of the Purchase Orders and within 24 hours after acceptance of such procurement inform the assigned audit team leader of the deliveries made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 noted the herein Part II audit Finding recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer faithfully adhere to all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund particularly the submission of the annual local disaster risk reduction management investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2020 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012-002 to support its legality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 noted the herein Part II audit Finding recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and reported. Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 noted the herein Part II audit Finding recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should instruct official concerned to submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 noted the herein Part II audit Finding recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 noted the herein Part II audit Finding recommendation.

Submit the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS ARMM.

a. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as
this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and
determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial
responsibilities;
b. Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count
on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor
immediately thereafter; and
c. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger
Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete
and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

Recommendation
a. Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on
Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of
cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report
thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books
of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth
day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

We recommend that Management strictly observe adherence to the provisions of
Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (i) the MPDO prepares
Annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and annual work
and financial plan where disbursements of LDRRMF is strictly based; (ii)
transfer to a special trust fund the unexpended LDRRMF for the purpose of
supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five
year.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in
COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote
Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the
proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the
implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct
advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater
role for men in raising a family.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste
Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an
arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner
in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of
results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommend that management particularly to the members of the Bids and
Award Committee, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations. That in cases
of procurement method applied is not within the approved APP, the BAC through
a resolution must recommend to the head of the procuring agency the specific
alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval,
before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1
and 48.3 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in coordination with
the BAC Chairman, facilitate the timely submission of all required documents
associated to infrastructure projects implemented by administration. Submit
the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling
for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS
ARMM.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various
Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology
Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project
requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part II recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part II recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2017 noted the herein Part II recommendation.

We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in coordination with the BAC Chairman, facilitate the timely submission of all required documents associated to infrastructure projects implemented by administration. Submit the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS ARMM.

We  recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public  notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a monthly report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan.  Develop an Action/Implementation Plan.  Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommended that:
The municipal accountant should be required to record all consummated procurement thru the inventory account and properly follow the steps of recording incoming and out-going supplies and materials in a perpetual method of inventory keeping.  This will prevent purchases in bulk be charged to outright expense; and
b. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer must maintain Stock Cards as required for in Section 119 of NGAS Manual Vol. I for local government units.

We recommended that:
The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise,  we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end.  Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996. 

Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

We recommend that the management should formalize a policy for the granting of financial assistance to its constituents and the various NGOs taking into consideration the need and the budget for the year.  Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance should be extended only once to the same person/ NGO’s.

The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.

observations.recommendation:
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities. 
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that copies of Purchased Orders/Contracts and Acceptance Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end.  Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.  Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We strongly recommended the reclassification of the Cash Advances transaction referred thereto to the appropriate account/s in compliance to COA Accounting Circular No. 2006 – 001 dated November 9, 2006.

We recommended that:
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities;
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter; and
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management submit its GAD Plan and accomplishment reports to facilitate timely evaluation on the implementation of GAD related programs/projects and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.

We recommend that the management should formalize a policy for the granting of financial assistance to its constituents and the various NGOs taking into consideration the need and the budget for the year.  Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance should be extended only once to the same person/ NGO’s.

We recommend that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

The municipal treasurer should immediately deposit the cash in vault amounting to P118,960.33; and
Management should follow strictly Section 32, Volume 1 of NGAS (LGUs) Manual and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collection intact with the authorized depository bank to avoid exposing the funds from possible losses through theft or robbery.

We recommend that depreciation expenses be provided and the related accumulated depreciation accounts for all depreciable assets be updated in accordance with the NGAS Manual, for a realistic presentation of the value thereof in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, the management must prepare Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in support of disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC, as stated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.  Management must also evaluate the basic needs as against the capability to timely procure and distribute goods or deliver on time, needed social/health services during crisis time.  This effective and efficient delivery of immediate concerns during crisis time can best be addressed following the guidelines and procedures in the adoption of an appropriate disaster preparedness plan incorporating all crisis preparedness and prevention activities subject to the declaration of imminent danger of calamity by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.
Management noted the herein audit recommendation.
